title: Avatar: Fire and Ash Dominates Box Office for Fifth Consecutive Week
text: James Cameron's latest Avatar installment has maintained its position atop the box office for five straight weeks, a milestone last achieved by The Way of Water in 2022. The film earned $14.4 million in its sixth weekend, bringing its domestic total to $363.5 million. Despite strong performance, it trails The Way of Water's $564.6 million at the same point—a $200 million difference. Globally, Fire and Ash has grossed $1.319 billion, entering the top 25 highest-grossing films worldwide. The film remains on track to surpass the $400 million domestic threshold and currently ranks fourth among 2025 releases. This represents only the fifth film in the 2000s to lead the box office for five consecutive weeks. The exclusive club includes the original Avatar and first Black Panther film, with The Way of Water holding the current record at seven weeks. Fire and Ash pursues Zootopia 2, which sits comfortably at number nine on the all-time worldwide chart with $1.703 billion. The Disney animated sequel continues drawing audiences domestically as well.
